title: Savi launches a consumer app aimed at realistic AI scams
friendlyTitle: Savi wants to help people handle scary AI scams
summary: TechCrunch reports that Savi Security raised $7 million in seed funding and launched an iPhone and Android app designed to help consumers respond to realistic AI scams, including impersonation and ransom-style calls. The product targets a growing consumer-security problem as voice and image generation improve.
friendlySummary: Savi launched a phone app meant to help consumers deal with realistic AI scams, such as fake kidnapping calls or impersonation attempts.
whyItMatters: AI fraud is becoming personal, urgent, and emotionally manipulative. Tools that help families verify suspicious calls or messages could become part of everyday digital safety, alongside password managers and two-factor authentication.
friendlyWhyItMatters: AI scams can sound very real and frightening. Families may need simple tools and plans to check whether an emergency call is genuine.
